steps.title: Identify the core contrast
steps.content: The strongest clue is the “but” that follows, which acknowledges journalists “allow their own views to creep into their reporting” versus trying to “be neutral.” This structure reveals what the politicians claim.

steps.title: Match the target meaning
steps.content:
The blank describes what politicians claim journalists are — the "but" that follows concedes that some journalists do let their own views "creep into" reporting, which tells us the claim relates to personal views influencing reporting. Substitute each choice:
  • "sensitive": easily affected or emotionally reactive — this describes a personality trait, not the problem of personal views distorting reporting; the passage is about impartiality, not emotional fragility. ✘
  • "biased": showing an unfair preference influenced by personal views — directly matched by the concession that journalists let "their own views creep into their reporting." ✔
  • "misinformed": having incorrect information — this suggests journalists lack facts, not that they inject personal opinions; the passage is about neutrality, not accuracy of facts. ✘
  • "irrelevant": not connected to the matter at hand — politicians attacking journalists as irrelevant contradicts their very concern about journalistic influence. ✘
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
